question: Will Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange exceed 9000 USD/tonne on May 31, 2026?
reason: Indonesia's significant nickel mining quota cuts have led to substantial price increases.
evidence: Indonesia reduced its 2026 nickel mining quota by approximately 34% to 205 million wet metric tonnes, down from 379 million in 2025.
This reduction has led to immediate increases in global nickel prices.
The London Metal Exchange (LME) nickel prices have risen sharply following the announcement.
Analysts expect the global nickel surplus to narrow significantly in 2026 due to these cuts.
The International Nickel Study Group (INSG) forecasts a 32,000-tonne deficit for 2026, the first since 2021.
The Indonesian government aims to align mining output with domestic processing capacity and smelter needs.
The reduced supply is expected to support higher nickel prices in the global market.
The LME nickel price reached a two-year high in April 2026, influenced by these supply constraints.
